reading_plan.reading_goal: Reconstruct the research evolution trajectory of developing Blackjack mutations to improve on-target activities of increased fidelity SpCas9 variants with 5'G-extended sgRNAs. Identify the initial problem (off-target effects of SpCas9 and reduced activity of high-fidelity variants with extended sgRNAs), constraints (structural interactions between sgRNA and Cas9), hypotheses (mutations in the loop region can restore activity), experimental designs (mutagenesis, activity assays, specificity tests), observations (activity restoration, maintained fidelity), failures/partial results (some variants not improved), revisions (iterative mutation combinations), validations (in cell lines, off-target analysis), and limitations (context-dependent effects).

Design of Blackjack mutations: specific amino acid substitutions in the loop region.
Iterative testing: activity assays with different sgRNAs and targets.
Observations: restoration of on-target activity while maintaining high fidelity.
Failures or partial results: some variants did not improve activity or lost fidelity.
Revisions: combining mutations to optimize activity.
Validation: testing in human cell lines, off-target analysis using GUIDE-seq or similar.
Limitations: context-dependence, need for further optimization.

gist_memory.paper_problem_memory: Increased fidelity SpCas9 variants (eSpCas9, SpCas9-HF1, HypaSpCas9, evoSpCas9, HeFSpCas9) reduce off-target effects but have diminished activity with 5'G-extended sgRNAs (21G-sgRNAs), limiting their target space. The hypothesis is that a structural cap formed by Glu1007 and Tyr1013 on a surface loop clashes with the 5' extension, and removing this cap via mutations could restore activity while maintaining or improving fidelity.

section_gists.heading: Blackjack mutations improve the on-target activities of increased fidelity variants of SpCas9 with 5'G-extended sgRNAs
section_gists.gist: Introduction outlines the problem: increased fidelity SpCas9 variants have reduced activity with 5'G-extended sgRNAs, restricting target space. Hypothesis: a structural cap (Glu1007, Tyr1013) on a loop hinders 5' extensions; removing it may restore activity and increase fidelity. Goal: develop variants that work with 21G-sgRNAs while maintaining high fidelity.
process_signals: Problem identification: high-fidelity variants ineffective with 5'G-extended sgRNAs
Structural hypothesis: capping of sgRNA 5' end by Glu1007 and Tyr1013 loop affects activity
Rationale: removing cap by mutation could allow 5' extension without clash, similar to SpCas9-HF1 design but targeting RNA strand
failures_or_limits: Increased fidelity variants have limited target space due to 5'G-extension intolerance
Many variants reported to work less efficiently in RNP form

section_gists.heading: Results
section_gists.gist: Initial mutants: replaced Glu1007/Tyr1013 with glycine (inactive) or deleted loop region (active with 21G-sgRNAs). Screened 13 targets, selected best deletion variant (B-SpCas9-HF1: L1004-K1014 replaced with two glycines). Introduced Blackjack mutations into other high-fidelity variants (eSpCas9, HypaSpCas9, evoSpCas9, HeFSpCas9) and tested activity with 20G/21G-sgRNAs. B-eSpCas9 and B-SpCas9-HF1 showed improved activity with 21G-sgRNAs. Further optimization: combined Blackjack mutations with additional mutations (e.g., R691A) to create eSpCas9-plus and SpCas9-HF1-plus, which matched or exceeded parental on-target activity and fidelity, and worked with 21G-sgRNAs. Tested in EGFP disruption assays, GUIDE-seq for off-targets, and RNP delivery.
process_signals: Initial glycine substitution mutant inactive; deletion mutants active
Screened 13 targets to identify suitable ones for further testing
Created 16 deletion mutants, tested with selected targets, chose best candidate (B-SpCas9-HF1)
Blackjack mutations introduced into other high-fidelity variants
Combined Blackjack with additional mutations (e.g., R691A) to optimize activity
eSpCas9-plus and SpCas9-HF1-plus showed matching on-target activity and fidelity with 20G-sgRNAs, and retained activity with 21G-sgRNAs
GUIDE-seq confirmed high fidelity of plus variants
Plus variants effective in RNP form
methods: Site-directed mutagenesis (deletions, substitutions) in loop region (aa 1003-1017)
EGFP disruption assay for on-target activity
failures_or_limits: Some deletion mutants not as effective
Some variants (e.g., B-HypaSpCas9, B-evoSpCas9) not fully characterized or less improved
Context-dependent activity: some targets still show low activity for certain variants

section_gists.heading: Discussion
section_gists.gist: Three Blackjack variants (eSpCas9-plus, SpCas9-HF1-plus, and B-SpCas9) are likely to replace corresponding high-fidelity variants. They maintain high fidelity and work with 21G-sgRNAs, expanding target space. Plus variants show similar activity whether delivered as plasmids or RNP. Limitations: some targets remain refractory; further optimization may be needed for specific applications. The structural basis of fidelity enhancement by 5' extensions is discussed.
process_signals: eSpCas9-plus and SpCas9-HF1-plus are superior to parental variants
Blackjack mutations enable use of existing pooled sgRNA libraries with higher specificity
RNP delivery works well with plus variants
Some targets still show low activity, indicating context dependence
failures_or_limits: Not all targets are equally improved; some remain low activity
Further optimization may be needed for certain applications
Structural explanation for fidelity increase by 5' extensions still incomplete

Activity measured by EGFP disruption in N2a.dd-EGFP cells. Off-targets assessed by GUIDE-seq in HEK293 cells. RNP delivery tested by electroporation.

global_hypotheses_about_storyline: The research evolved from identifying the problem (high-fidelity variants fail with 5'G-extended sgRNAs) to a structural hypothesis (cap by Glu1007/Tyr1013 loop) to iterative mutagenesis and screening to find optimal loop deletions (Blackjack mutations).
Initial failure of point mutations led to focus on deletions, with progressive refinement to a minimal two-glycine linker.
Blackjack mutations were then combined with existing high-fidelity mutations to create 'plus' variants that restore activity with 21G-sgRNAs while maintaining or improving fidelity.
Validation included on-target activity, off-target analysis (GUIDE-seq), and RNP delivery, demonstrating practical utility.
uncertainties_to_resolve: Exact structural mechanism by which Blackjack mutations increase fidelity is not fully elucidated.
Why some targets remain refractory to improvement even with Blackjack mutations.
Whether Blackjack mutations affect other properties (e.g., PAM specificity, stability) not tested here.
Long-term effects in therapeutic contexts not explored.

events.summary: Increased fidelity SpCas9 variants have reduced activity with 5'G-extended sgRNAs, limiting target space.
Hypothesis that a structural cap formed by Glu1007 and Tyr1013 on a surface loop clashes with 5' extension, and removing it could restore activity.
Designed mutants: replaced Glu1007/Tyr1013 with glycine, or deleted loop region (aa 1004-1014) replaced with two glycines.
Tested mutants in EGFP-disruption assay; deletion mutants active with 21G-sgRNAs, glycine mutant inactive.
Screened 13 target sites with deletion mutants and selected targets for further testing.
Created 16 further deletion mutants between aa 1003-1017, varying loop removal and replacement lengths.
Selected best candidate (B-SpCas9-HF1) with highest on-target activity with 20G-sgRNAs and improvement with 21G-sgRNAs.

chunk_events.notes: Chunk covers introduction of problem, hypothesis, initial mutant design, screening, and selection of B-SpCas9-HF1, plus extension to other variants.

events.summary: Blackjack mutations increase target-selectivity of parent SpCas9 variants with 20G-sgRNAs.
Blackjack mutations increase fidelity of increased fidelity nucleases.
Rationale to create plus variants: compensate for fidelity increase by restoring some parental mutations to WT.
Constructed four and seven candidates from Blackjack-eSpCas9 and Blackjack-SpCas9-HF1 lacking one or two parental mutations.
Tested residue-reverted variants for SpCas9-HF1 on five targets with 20G-sgRNAs; all except A497N reversion showed increased activity.
Western blotting showed Blackjack mutations do not alter expression levels; plus variants expressed comparably to parents.

chunk_events.notes: Chunk describes the design and testing of plus variants to match fidelity of parental high-fidelity nucleases while retaining Blackjack benefits.

events.summary: Tested on-target activities of eSpCas9-plus and SpCas9-HF1-plus with 20G- and 21G-sgRNAs using EGFP disruption assay.
Measured on-target activities of plus variants across 23 endogenous target sites in human VEGFA or FANCF loci using amplicon resequencing (NGS).
Found that with 21G-sgRNAs, eSpCas9-plus demonstrates 88% and SpCas9-HF1-plus 82% of WT SpCas9 activity, matching their 20G-sgRNA relative activities.
Compared fidelity of plus variants to parental variants using GUIDE-seq on seven target sites with 20G-sgRNAs.
GUIDE-seq results showed that plus variants have fidelity closely matching parental variants, with eSpCas9-plus cleaving slightly less and SpCas9-HF1-plus slightly more off-target sites.
Tested plus variants in RNP form using EGFP disruption assay on 31 sequences with in vitro transcribed sgRNAs.
Found that only plus variants demonstrate high activities with 21G-sgRNAs in RNP form, up to 23-fold higher than parental variants.
Demonstrated practical advantage of Blackjack variants by integrating EGFP cassette into mouse Shadoo locus using 21G-sgRNAs, where no 20G-sgRNA targets were available.

chunk_events.notes: Chunk contains extensive experimental validation of plus variants, including on-target activity, fidelity, RNP delivery, and practical application.

events.summary: The plus variants are effective when transfected in pre-assembled RNP form, as shown by EGFP-disruption assays on targets with 5' non-G-, G-, or GG-nucleotides.
Blackjack variants facilitate modification of the endogenous Sprn gene at the 5' coding region and are effective with sgRNAs expressed from a single-copy lentivirus.
eSpCas9-plus, SpCas9-HF1-plus, and B-SpCas9 are superior variants of eSpCas9, SpCas-HF1, and WT SpCas9, respectively, with identical fidelity and on-target activity to their original counterparts.
The plus variants provide 20G-level editing with 21G-sgRNAs, advantageous when sgRNA is transcribed from a DNA template and target space is limited.
21G-sgRNAs could generally be employed instead of 20G-sgRNAs for almost all targets to provide higher specificity editing with WT SpCas9.

Incorporation of Blackjack mutations into an increased fidelity variant of SpCas9 that is not fully compatible with 5' modified sgRNAs would considerably increase its available target space by making them compatible with 21G-sgRNAs.

chunk_events.notes: This chunk contains experimental validation (RNP delivery, endogenous gene modification) and analysis of the plus variants' performance, fidelity, and advantages with 21G-sgRNAs.

events.summary: EGFP-disruption assay used to measure on-target activity of SpCas9 variants in N2a.dd-EGFP cells.
On-target activity measured on N2a.EGFP cell line for more sensitive detection of intrinsic nuclease activities.
Mismatch screens and 21G-sgRNA screens performed in N2a.dd-EGFP cells to assess off-target activities.
Western blot performed to analyze SpCas9 variant expression levels in N2a.dd-EGFP cells.
Indel analysis by next-generation sequencing (NGS) used to quantify editing efficiency in HEK293 cells.
EGFP-disruption assay with RNP used to test SpCas9 variants delivered as ribonucleoprotein complexes.
GUIDE-seq performed to detect off-target cleavage sites for SpCas9 variants on thirteen target sites.
TIDE method applied for analyzing mutations and determining their frequency in cell populations.

chunk_events.notes: This chunk describes detailed experimental methods for assessing on-target and off-target activities of SpCas9 variants, including EGFP disruption, NGS, GUIDE-seq, and RNP delivery. These methods are central to validating the Blackjack mutations and plus variants.

events.summary: Cells were co-transfected with SpCas9 variant expression plasmid and mCherry coding plasmid, and analyzed 5 days post-transfection by flow cytometry.
Transfection efficacy was calculated via mCherry expressing cells, and TIDE method was applied for analyzing mutations and determining their frequency.
Statistical tests (Paired-samples Student's t-test or Wilcoxon Signed Ranks test) were used to compare SpCas9 variants, with normality tested by Shapiro-Wilk.
Data availability: plasmids available at Addgene, sequences in Supplementary information, raw data in Supplementary Data, deep-sequencing data submitted to NCBI SRA under accession PRJNA593843.

chunk_events.notes: This chunk contains methods details (transfection, analysis, statistics) and data availability statements, but no new research evolution events beyond standard experimental procedures and data deposition.

event_graph.paper_main_problem: Increased fidelity SpCas9 variants have reduced activity with 5'G-extended sgRNAs, limiting target space.

canonical_events.summary: Increased fidelity SpCas9 variants (eSpCas9, SpCas9-HF1, HypaSpCas9, evoSpCas9, HeFSpCas9) have reduced activity with 5'G-extended sgRNAs (21G-sgRNAs), limiting target space.
Hypothesis: a structural cap formed by Glu1007 and Tyr1013 on a surface loop clashes with the 5' extension; removing it could restore activity.
Designed initial mutants: replaced Glu1007/Tyr1013 with glycine, or deleted loop region (aa 1004-1014) replaced with two glycines.
Tested mutants in EGFP-disruption assay; deletion mutants active with 21G-sgRNAs, glycine mutant inactive.
Screened 13 target sites with deletion mutants and selected targets for further testing.
Created 16 further deletion mutants between aa 1003-1017, varying loop removal and replacement lengths.
Selected best candidate (B-SpCas9-HF1) with highest on-target activity with 20G-sgRNAs and improvement with 21G-sgRNAs.
Blackjack mutations increase target-selectivity and fidelity of parent SpCas9 variants with 20G-sgRNAs.
Rationale to create plus variants: compensate for fidelity increase by restoring some parental mutations to WT.
Constructed and tested residue-reverted variants from Blackjack-eSpCas9 and Blackjack-SpCas9-HF1; all except A497N reversion showed increased activity.
Western blotting showed Blackjack mutations do not alter expression levels; plus variants expressed comparably to parents.
Tested on-target activities of eSpCas9-plus and SpCas9-HF1-plus with 20G- and 21G-sgRNAs using EGFP disruption assay; plus variants show greatly enhanced activity with 21G-sgRNAs.
Measured on-target activities of plus variants across 23 endogenous target sites in human VEGFA or FANCF loci using amplicon resequencing (NGS).
With 21G-sgRNAs, eSpCas9-plus demonstrates 88% and SpCas9-HF1-plus 82% of WT SpCas9 activity, matching their 20G-sgRNA relative activities.
Compared fidelity of plus variants to parental variants using GUIDE-seq on seven target sites with 20G-sgRNAs.
GUIDE-seq results showed plus variants have fidelity closely matching parental variants, with eSpCas9-plus cleaving slightly less and SpCas9-HF1-plus slightly more off-target sites.
Tested plus variants in RNP form using EGFP disruption assay on 31 sequences with in vitro transcribed sgRNAs.
Only plus variants demonstrate high activities with 21G-sgRNAs in RNP form, up to 23-fold higher than parental variants.

edges.reason: Hypothesis leads to design of initial mutants.
Initial test results lead to screening of targets.
Screening identifies need for more deletion mutants.
Further mutants are analyzed to select best candidate.
Best candidate (B-SpCas9-HF1) is characterized for selectivity and fidelity.
Increased fidelity leads to rationale for plus variants.
Rationale leads to construction and testing of revertants.
Expression levels of variants are validated.
Selected plus variants are tested for on-target activity.
On-target activity is further measured on endogenous sites.
Plus variants are tested in RNP form.

must_cover_points: Problem: high-fidelity variants fail with 5'G-extended sgRNAs.
Structural hypothesis: Glu1007/Tyr1013 cap clashes with 5' extension.
Initial mutant design and testing: glycine mutant inactive, deletion mutants active.
Blackjack mutations increase fidelity and selectivity.
Rationale for plus variants: restore some parental mutations to compensate for fidelity increase.
Construction and testing of revertants; A497N reversion exception.
Plus variants show enhanced activity with 21G-sgRNAs.
Quantitative on-target activity: 88% and 82% of WT with 21G-sgRNAs.
GUIDE-seq confirms fidelity matching parental variants.
RNP delivery shows high activity with 21G-sgRNAs, up to 23-fold higher.
Practical advantage demonstrated in Shadoo locus integration.
Conclusion: plus variants are superior replacements.

self_critic.summary: The candidate trajectory has several issues: irrelevant evidence in multiple steps, overly broad supporting_event_ids, missing steps (K04 initial mutant testing, K16 GUIDE-seq analysis), and phase mislabeling. Revision should remove irrelevant evidence, limit supporting_event_ids to the primary event covered, add missing steps, and correct phases.

source.title: Blackjack mutations improve the on-target activities of increased fidelity variants of SpCas9 with 5′G-extended sgRNAs

initial_request.research_problem: Increased fidelity SpCas9 variants have reduced activity with 5'G-extended sgRNAs, limiting target space.
initial_request.target_object: SpCas9 variants (eSpCas9, SpCas9-HF1, HypaSpCas9, evoSpCas9, HeFSpCas9)
initial_request.known_context: Increased fidelity variants were developed to reduce off-target activity but show diminished activity with 5'-extended sgRNAs (21G-sgRNAs).
input_data: Known mutations in increased fidelity variants
quantifiable_goals: Achieve on-target activity with 21G-sgRNAs comparable to 20G-sgRNAs

evolution_trajectory.state_before: Increased fidelity SpCas9 variants (eSpCas9, SpCas9-HF1, HypaSpCas9, evoSpCas9, HeFSpCas9) have reduced activity with 5'G-extended sgRNAs (21G-sgRNAs), limiting target space.
evolution_trajectory.gap_or_uncertainty: The structural basis for reduced activity with 21G-sgRNAs is unknown.
evolution_trajectory.hypothesis: A structural cap formed by Glu1007 and Tyr1013 on a surface loop clashes with the 5' extension; removing it could restore activity.
evolution_trajectory.decision: Propose to remove the cap by mutation to make space for the 5' G-extension.
evolution_trajectory.observation: Glu1007 and Tyr1013 are connected via a surface loop and may cap the 5' end of the sgRNA.
evolution_trajectory.next_step_reason: Test the hypothesis by designing mutants that remove the cap.

evidence.quote_or_span: most of the increased fidelity nucleases can routinely be used only with fully matching 20-nucleotide-long spacers (20G-sgRNAs)
they possess diminished activity with 5'-extended sgRNAs
We proposed that it might result from a capping of the 5' end of the sgRNA by Glu1007 and Tyr1013, which are connected via a surface loop
removing the cap by mutation would make space for a 5' G-extension of the sgRNA without clashing with the polypeptide chain

the incorporation of Blackjack mutations into an increased fidelity variant of SpCas9 that is not fully compatible with 5' modified sgRNAs and using it either alone or in combination with a genome- or epigenome-editor would considerable increase its available target space by making them compatible with 21G-sgRNAs.

evolution_trajectory.state_before: Deletion mutants active with 21G-sgRNAs, but need to assess performance across targets.
evolution_trajectory.gap_or_uncertainty: Which targets show improved performance with deletion mutants?
evolution_trajectory.hypothesis: Some targets will be suitable for detecting improved performance.
evolution_trajectory.decision: Screen 13 target sites with the two deletion mutants, WT, and SpCas9-HF1.
evolution_trajectory.observation: Identified some targets that appeared suitable for easy detection of possibly improved performance.
evolution_trajectory.next_step_reason: Create further deletion mutants to optimize the loop removal.

evidence.quote_or_span: we screened 13 target sites with the two deletion mutants created, along with the WT and SpCas9-HF1 (Supplementary Fig. 1b) and identified some targets that appeared suitable for easy detection of possibly improved performance

they would be excellent substitutes for eSpCas9 and SpCas9-HF1 in all types of applications for which the latter two have been preferentially used but with the added advantage of providing 20G-level editing with 21G-sgRNAs as well.

evolution_trajectory.state_before: Initial deletion mutants show promise but need optimization.
evolution_trajectory.gap_or_uncertainty: Optimal loop deletion length and position unknown.
evolution_trajectory.hypothesis: Varying the deletion length and position will yield a mutant with higher activity.
evolution_trajectory.decision: Create 16 further deletion mutants between aa 1003-1017, varying loop removal and replacement lengths.
evolution_trajectory.observation: Created 16 further deletion mutant candidates.
evolution_trajectory.next_step_reason: Test and select the best candidate based on on-target activity.

evidence.quote_or_span: we created 16 further deletion mutant candidates in that region, specifically between aa. 1003 and aa. 1017, by completely removing or exchanging segments of various lengths harboring the loop

evolution_trajectory.state_before: 16 further deletion mutants created.
evolution_trajectory.gap_or_uncertainty: Which mutant has the best balance of activity with 20G- and 21G-sgRNAs?
evolution_trajectory.hypothesis: The best candidate will exhibit highest on-target activity with 20G-sgRNAs and highest improvement with 21G-sgRNAs.
evolution_trajectory.decision: Select the best candidate (B-SpCas9-HF1) based on activity criteria.
evolution_trajectory.observation: The best candidate was considered to be the one exhibiting the highest on-target activity with 20G-sgRNAs and demonstrating the highest improvement with 21G-sgRNAs. This variant was named Blackjack-SpCas9-HF1 (B-SpCas9-HF1).
evolution_trajectory.next_step_reason: Characterize the selected variant for target-selectivity and fidelity.

evidence.quote_or_span: The best candidate was considered to be the one exhibiting the highest on-target activity with 20G-sgRNAs and demonstrating the highest improvement with 21G-sgRNAs. This variant was named Blackjack-SpCas9-HF1 (B-SpCas9-HF1)
we created 16 further deletion mutant candidates in that region, specifically between aa. 1003 and aa. 1017, by completely removing or exchanging segments of various lengths harboring the loop

evolution_trajectory.state_before: B-SpCas9-HF1 selected.
evolution_trajectory.gap_or_uncertainty: Do Blackjack mutations affect target-selectivity and fidelity?
evolution_trajectory.hypothesis: Blackjack mutations increase target-selectivity and fidelity of parent SpCas9 variants with 20G-sgRNAs.
evolution_trajectory.decision: Assess target-selectivity and fidelity using EGFP-disruption assays with perfectly matching and partially mismatching 20G-sgRNAs.
evolution_trajectory.observation: Blackjack mutations increase the target-selectivity and fidelity of their respective parent SpCas9 variants.
evolution_trajectory.next_step_reason: Blackjack mutations increase fidelity, which may reduce on-target activity; design plus variants to compensate.

evidence.quote_or_span: Blackjack mutations increase the target-selectivity of their respective parent SpCas9 variants. EGFP-disruption activities with perfectly matching 20G-sgRNAs.
Blackjack mutations increase the fidelity of their respective parent SpCas9 variants. EGFP-disruption activities with partially mismatching 20G-sgRNAs.

evolution_trajectory.state_before: Plus variant design rationale established.
evolution_trajectory.gap_or_uncertainty: Which reversions increase activity?
evolution_trajectory.hypothesis: Most reversions will increase on-target activity, but some may not.
evolution_trajectory.decision: Construct four and seven candidates from Blackjack-eSpCas9 and Blackjack-SpCas9-HF1, respectively, lacking one or two original parental mutations at a time, and test on-target activity with 20G-sgRNAs.
evolution_trajectory.observation: All new candidates exhibit increased on-target activity with 20G-sgRNAs compared to B-SpCas9-HF1 except that in which A497 was reverted.
evolution_trajectory.next_step_reason: Select the best plus variants (eSpCas9-plus and SpCas9-HF1-plus) and test with 21G-sgRNAs.

evidence.quote_or_span: we constructed four and seven candidates from Blackjack-eSpCas9 and Blackjack-SpCas9-HF1, respectively, lacking one or two “original parental” mutations at a time
All new candidates exhibit increased on-target activity with 20G-sgRNAs compared to B-SpCas9-HF1 except that in which A497 was reverted

The best candidate was considered to be the one exhibiting the highest on-target activity with 20G-sgRNAs and demonstrating the highest improvement with 21G-sgRNAs. This variant was named Blackjack-SpCas9-HF1 (B-SpCas9-HF1)

evolution_trajectory.state_before: Plus variants show increased activity with 20G-sgRNAs.
evolution_trajectory.gap_or_uncertainty: Do plus variants show enhanced activity with 21G-sgRNAs while maintaining fidelity?
evolution_trajectory.hypothesis: eSpCas9-plus and SpCas9-HF1-plus will show greatly enhanced on-target activity with 21G-sgRNAs and identical fidelity/target-selectivity compared to parental variants.
evolution_trajectory.decision: Test on-target activities of eSpCas9-plus and SpCas9-HF1-plus with 20G- and 21G-sgRNAs using EGFP disruption assay.
evolution_trajectory.observation: eSpCas9-plus and SpCas9-HF1-plus show greatly enhanced on-target activity with 21G-sgRNAs and identical fidelity/target-selectivity compared to eSpCas9 and SpCas9-HF1, respectively.
evolution_trajectory.next_step_reason: Validate on-target activity on endogenous sites and assess fidelity with GUIDE-seq.

evidence.quote_or_span: eSpCas9-plus and SpCas9-HF1-plus show greatly enhanced on-target activity with 21G-sgRNAs and identical fidelity/target-selectivity compared to eSpCas9 and SpCas9-HF1, respectively, as assessed by EGFP disruption
6d) demonstrated highly increased on-target activities; reaching 90% of that with 20G-sgRNAs on the same targets, in contrast to their parent variants demonstrating only 10 and 16%, respectively.

evolution_trajectory.state_before: Plus variants show enhanced activity in EGFP disruption assay.
evolution_trajectory.gap_or_uncertainty: Do plus variants maintain high activity on endogenous targets?
evolution_trajectory.hypothesis: Plus variants will show high on-target activity across endogenous sites with 21G-sgRNAs.
evolution_trajectory.decision: Measure on-target activities of plus variants across 23 endogenous target sites in human VEGFA or FANCF loci using amplicon resequencing (NGS).
evolution_trajectory.observation: On-target activities measured across 23 endogenous target sites.
evolution_trajectory.next_step_reason: Quantify the relative activity compared to WT SpCas9.

evidence.quote_or_span: On-target activities of SpCas9 variants across 23 endogenous target sites within the human VEGFA or FANCF loci targeted with d 20G- or e 21G-sgRNAs, measured by amplicon resequencing.

evolution_trajectory.state_before: Endogenous on-target activity data collected.
evolution_trajectory.gap_or_uncertainty: How do plus variants compare to WT SpCas9 with 21G-sgRNAs?
evolution_trajectory.hypothesis: Plus variants will achieve a high percentage of WT activity with 21G-sgRNAs.
evolution_trajectory.decision: Calculate relative activity of plus variants compared to WT SpCas9 with 21G-sgRNAs.
evolution_trajectory.observation: With 21G-sgRNAs eSpCas9-plus demonstrates 88%, while SpCas9-HF1-plus exhibits 82% of the value of WT SpCas9 with 21G-sgRNA.
evolution_trajectory.next_step_reason: Assess fidelity of plus variants using GUIDE-seq.

evidence.quote_or_span: with 21G-sgRNAs eSpCas9-plus demonstrates 88%, while SpCas9-HF1-plus exhibits 82% of the value of WT SpCas9 with 21G-sgRNA

evolution_trajectory.state_before: Plus variants show high on-target activity.
evolution_trajectory.gap_or_uncertainty: Do plus variants maintain fidelity comparable to parental variants?
evolution_trajectory.hypothesis: Plus variants will have fidelity closely matching parental variants.
evolution_trajectory.decision: Compare fidelity of plus variants to parental variants using GUIDE-seq on seven target sites with 20G-sgRNAs.
evolution_trajectory.observation: GUIDE-seq performed on seven target sites.
evolution_trajectory.next_step_reason: Analyze GUIDE-seq results to confirm fidelity.

evidence.quote_or_span: We also wanted to compare the fidelity of these nucleases by GUIDE-seq. In HEK293.EGFP cells, we selected seven target sites that can be targeted by 20G-sgRNAs

evolution_trajectory.state_before: Fidelity of plus variants confirmed.
evolution_trajectory.gap_or_uncertainty: Are plus variants effective when delivered as RNPs?
evolution_trajectory.hypothesis: Plus variants will demonstrate high activities with 21G-sgRNAs in RNP form.
evolution_trajectory.decision: Test plus variants in RNP form using EGFP disruption assay on 31 sequences with in vitro transcribed sgRNAs.
evolution_trajectory.observation: Selected 31 sequences to assay for EGFP disruption by eSpCas9 and SpCas9-HF1 and by their plus variants delivered in RNP form.
evolution_trajectory.next_step_reason: Quantify the activity improvement in RNP form.

evidence.quote_or_span: we selected 31 sequences to assay for EGFP disruption by eSpCas9 and SpCas9-HF1 and by their plus variants delivered in RNP form
only the plus variants demonstrate high activities with 21G-sgRNAs in pre-assembled RNP form too, reaching up to 23-fold higher activities than their parental variants

evolution_trajectory.state_before: RNP activity data collected.
evolution_trajectory.gap_or_uncertainty: How much higher is the activity of plus variants in RNP form?
evolution_trajectory.hypothesis: Plus variants will show up to 23-fold higher activity than parental variants with 21G-sgRNAs in RNP form.
evolution_trajectory.decision: Compare activities of plus variants to parental variants in RNP form.
evolution_trajectory.observation: Only the plus variants demonstrate high activities with 21G-sgRNAs in pre-assembled RNP form too, reaching up to 23-fold higher activities than their parental variants.
evolution_trajectory.next_step_reason: Conclude that plus variants are superior replacements.

evidence.quote_or_span: only the plus variants demonstrate high activities with 21G-sgRNAs in pre-assembled RNP form too, reaching up to 23-fold higher activities than their parental variants

metrics: Relative activity compared to WT SpCas9
success_verification.final_conclusion: eSpCas9-plus and SpCas9-HF1-plus show greatly enhanced on-target activity with 21G-sgRNAs while maintaining fidelity matching parental variants, making them superior replacements.
